a:r.a:t: Across FY22–FY27, total ship spend narrows to an annualized ~$1.1B of outsourced work for DDG-51 and ~$3.0B for submarines.
FY2022–FY2027 SCN end cost (P-5c discretionary); OBBBA adds two FY2026 DDG-51s ($5.4B) and a second FY2026
DDG-51 adds incremental FY2026 AP/LLTM ($1.8B); submarines add none (supplier long-lead is already inside Basic Construction)
Removes GFE (propulsion, electronics, and ordnance) the Navy buys directly and furnished to Primes
GFE is non-addressable to outsourced Basic Construction players
e.g., plans, change orders, and other non-construction activities)
as these generally conducted by Primes
Removes construction the Primes perform in their own yards.
BC spend x the announced outside-yard share from DoD award place-of-performance data: ~25% DDG-51 (FY22 ships 22%), 34%
the P-10 EOQ base is vendor-purchased material, so Primes retain none — $0 removed; the full $1.0B flows to Outsourced BC.
no AP/LLTM stream exists, so there is nothing to remove.
Walk to Outsourced Basic Construction Spend ($B, cumulative FY22-FY27, FY26 $)

Sources: Navy SCN P-5c / P-40 budget justification, FY22–FY27, and PB27 FYDP outyears (FY28–FY31); FY26, PL 119-21 Sec. 20002; PB27 SCN, Columbia P-10 Strategic Outsourcing narrative; OUSD(C) Green Book Procurement deflators; Navy Shipbuilding Plan; PB27 SCN Exhibit P-10, LI 2122 (AP/LLTM Ship Construction EOQ); DoD contract award announcements (announced place-of-performance)
